metadescription.value: Under The Pole’s DEEPLIFE expedition to the Canary Islands aims to survey the region's towering forests of black coral. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Canaries Expedition

title.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Canaries Expedition

text.value: Just off the coast of Lanzarote, one of the Canary Islands, thick “forests” of black coral thrive in the deep waters of the ocean’s twilight zone. Between 30 and 200 metres below the surface, these marine animal forests are extremely difficult to study. Emmanuelle Périé-Bardout and Ghislain Bardout, co-founders of expert diving team Under The Pole, are working with scientists to better understand these “forests”. The data they collect will help conserve these important ecosystems for future generations.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue Hope Spot Champions Rili Djohani and Wira Sanjaya are working closely with communities in Bali to protect some of the world’s most biodiverse coral reefs.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Founder of the Coral Triangle Center, Rili Djohani has dedicated her life to protecting Nusa Penida's coral reefs. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Mission Blue: Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: Mount Logan Expedition Episode 1 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Deep within the ice on the summit of Mount Logan, Canada’s highest peak, are clues about the Earth’s climate and atmosphere spanning thousands of years. Scientist and high-altitude mountaineer Alison Criscitiello led the Rolex and National Geographic Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition to retrieve these layers of past climates. What they find will help us understand past changing climates, and what they mean for our future.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and National Geographic: Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Mountaineer Alison Criscitiello is expanding ice core research to new latitudes through her expedition to Mount Logan. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Rolex and National Geographic: Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Andes Mountains - Video

pageCaption.value: With support from Rolex and the National Geographic Society, climate scientists and Explorers Baker Perry and Tom Matthews endured snowstorms and sub-zero temperatures to install a state-of-the-art weather station on the summit of one of Peru’s highest mountains.

WaPageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Andes Mountains - Video

metadescription.value: Explorers Baker Perry and Tom Matthews have successfully installed a weather station in the high mountains of the Andes. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: The ecosystems of the Arctic seas remain mysterious even as the sea ice retreats due to climate change. To learn more, Under The Pole’s Ghislain Bardout and Emmanuelle Périé-Bardout led an expedition to Svalbard, supported by the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative. The explorers dived into the frigid waters in search of marine animal forests. These ecosystems are just as essential to life on Earth as forests on land, yet little is known about them.

WaPageTitle.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Svalbard Expedition - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Through the DEEPLIFE programme, researchers have set out to study the Arctic Ocean's unexplored marine animal forests. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Svalbard Expedition - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: At 16 years of age, while surfing the crystal waters of his home island in French Polynesia, Titouan Bernicot had an eye-opening experience that led him to dedicate his life to protecting the coral reefs he loves. He founded Coral Gardeners, kick-starting coral restoration on the island alongside his childhood friends. Today these “ocean kids” are inspiring a global community of reef defenders.

metadescription.value: Discover the story of Titouan Bernicot, founder of Coral Gardeners, who has dedicated his life to restoring coral reefs. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Coral Gardeners: Restoring the reef - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: 2021 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate Video: Luiz Rocha

pageCaption.value: A hundred metres or more beneath the ocean surface in the Maldives lies a twilight zone whose wondrous corals and strange life remain unexplored. In a pioneering diving expeditions, a leading expert in the study of fish, Luiz Rocha, plans to survey these deep reefs to find and describe new species and make the case for their protection.

metadescription.value: By exploring the Maldives' coral reefs, Luiz Rocha seeks to design Marine Protected Areas. Watch the video about his project on rolex.org.
